notes: Silent-ignore now at ~4.92 days and approaching the full 5-day horizon, simultaneously with a 5.42-day manual-commit drought. Counterargument for record (per user preference to consider the other side): absence of manual commits may simply reflect author travel, an offline period, or dev-time reserved for a larger batch update — it is not by itself evidence of strategic silence. Opposing considerations: (a) the 14-day window for PRED-105 closed five days ago; any good-faith adjudication would have landed a label (PASS/FAIL/REFINED) by now, and the SHA-anchored pre-registration means a legitimate pass would have high narrative value for the author; (b) predictions.html has been byte-identical across 7 polls — not just uncommitted but untouched, so this isn't a stash-and-rebase pattern; (c) the auto-pipeline's daily_review regeneration 15h before this poll kept registry_ratio_pct at 94.5 and refined at 4 without treating PRED-105 as a falsification, so the exclusion is pipeline-canonical and survives automatic regeneration (not dependent on manual intervention); (d) a109edf's file touch list (latest_hash / status_history / live_data / tracking.html only) is entirely routine — no prediction-adjudication logic ran. Next-poll escalation watch (unchanged from last poll, re-asserted): (1) goalpost-move on PRED-105 window text -> CRITICAL, (2) new WIN-070 with PRED-105-related claim -> CRITICAL per Step 5 hard-escalation, (3) any manual commit breaks ~130h+ streak (well past 5-day threshold) -> substantive minimum with canary/parameter diff required, (4) next daily_review auto-regen expected ~2026-04-23T07:06Z — if registry_ratio_pct moves or refined increments that would be new information.
